questions.question: What does the term 'Context Anxiety' refer to in the context of long-running agentic tasks?
options: A) A state where a model becomes overwhelmed by having too many agents in a single system.
B) A behavior where a model prematurely ends a task or loses coherence as it nears its context window limit.
C) The user's concern regarding the privacy of data stored within an LLM's context.
D) A failure mode where an agent forgets the initial system prompt due to a lack of memory.
questions.explanation: Context anxiety is specifically defined as the behavior where a model prematurely concludes tasks or loses coherence because it perceives its context window limit is approaching.

questions.question: Why did the researchers implement 'Context Resets' instead of mere 'Context Compaction'?
options: A) Compaction is technically impossible for modern transformer architectures.
B) Resets are faster to process because they require less computation than summarizing history.
C) Resets provide a clean slate that eliminates residual context anxiety better than compaction.
D) Resets allow the agent to switch to a different LLM provider mid-task.
questions.explanation: According to the key insights, context resets are more effective because they eliminate the residual context anxiety that persists when just compacting or summarizing the window.

questions.question: In the Multi-Agent Evaluator-Generator Loop, what is the primary reason for using a separate evaluator agent?
options: A) To ensure that the generator agent doesn't consume too many tokens.
B) To overcome the inherent bias of LLMs to praise their own mediocre work.
C) To allow the system to run on two different hardware servers simultaneously.
D) To provide a backup in case the generator agent fails to produce any output.
questions.explanation: The architecture separates the roles because LLMs are biased toward their own generated content; a separate, skeptical evaluator is needed to drive high-quality iterative improvements.

questions.question: How does the architecture address subjective qualities like 'good design' to ensure high-quality output?
options: A) By using a human-in-the-loop to approve every design iteration.
B) By utilizing a generator agent that has been specifically fine-tuned on art history.
C) By turning subjective qualities into concrete, gradable criteria for the evaluator agent.
D) By running the generator through a series of random aesthetic filters.
questions.explanation: The system achieves high-quality design by converting subjective qualities into gradable criteria, allowing the evaluator agent to objectively drive the iterative process.

questions.question: What mechanism is used to maintain progress during a 'Context Reset'?
options: A) Structured artifacts used to hand off state and progress to a fresh session.
B) A complete log of every chat message from the previous session.
C) A shared database that all agents write to in real-time.
D) Manual intervention where a user re-enters the current status of the project.
questions.explanation: A context reset involves clearing the window entirely and starting fresh while using structured artifacts to hand off the necessary state and progress information.
